actionUnits.condition: Automatic spread-on behavior via Update loop. MonoBehaviour (guid:1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) on this object matches the provided Fire script. There are no UnityEvent m_Calls and no public parameterless methods to call. Fire turns on automatically when onNeighbors >= onNeighborsThreshold (2). Requires that FireController (or equivalent) has already populated neighbors via Fire.FindNeighbors and at least two neighbors are currently on (NotifyNeighborState called). timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60 (Awake overrides timeToOn). If any neighbor is lower by > minNeighborDownDistance (3), downMultiplier=2 accelerates timeToOn. Expected: After sufficient time, isOn=true, UpdateFireState(true) runs, ParticleSystem on mainEffectGo (fileID:1849464941111748095) plays, neighbors are notified, and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1) is invoked.

Extinguish with water. This object has a BoxCollider (isTrigger=0) and no Rigidbody. To drive Fire.WaterHit(Vector3) repeatedly (public, requires a Vector3 normal), an external water stream/projectile with collision logic is needed. Hit intervals must remain below maxHitInterval (2s) and total accumulated hit time must reach totalHitTime (5s). Expected: isTurningOff true during hits; once currentHitTime >= 5s, the fire turns off (isOn=false), ParticleSystem stops, neighbors are notified, and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1) is invoked.

Single water hit stability test. Requires one call to Fire.WaterHit(Vector3) followed by no further hits for > maxHitInterval (2s). Expected: isTurningOff resets to false after currentHitInterval >= 2s and the fire remains on (no turn-off occurs and ParticleSystem keeps playing).
